Are you ready to treat yourself to a delicious and refreshing smoothie bowl that's perfect for the holiday season? Look no further than this Frosty Peppermint Delight Smoothie Bowl! 

Packed with festive flavors and served in eco-friendly coconut bowls, this recipe is sure to be a hit with your family and friends.

column3: Propagating Riverbank Grapes from Cuttings
column32: The best time to take cuttings is in late winter or early spring when the vine is still dormant. → Look for healthy, disease-free vines. Select stems that are at least one year old and have a diameter of about 1/4 inch. → Using sharp pruning shears, cut 6-8 inch sections of the vine. Ensure that each cutting has at least 2-3 buds. → Remove any leaves from the lower half of the cuttings. This helps reduce moisture loss and the risk of rot. → Dip the cut end of each cutting in rooting hormone to encourage root development. This step can improve success rates but is not strictly necessary. → Fill your containers with potting soil. Make a hole in the soil and insert the cutting about 2-3 inches deep, ensuring at least one bud is below the soil surface. Space the cuttings about 4-6 inches apart if using a larger container. → Water the cuttings thoroughly after planting. The soil should be moist but not soggy. → Cover the containers with plastic bags or place them in a humidity dome to maintain moisture levels. Ensure the bags are not touching the cuttings directly. → Place the containers in a bright location, but avoid direct sunlight which can overheat the cuttings. A spot with indirect light is ideal. → Check the soil regularly to ensure it stays moist. Remove the plastic covering occasionally to allow for air circulation. → After 4-8 weeks, gently tug on the cuttings to check for resistance, which indicates root development. → Once the cuttings have a strong root system, transplant them into larger pots or directly into the garden. Choose a sunny location with well-draining soil.
column33:
Care After Transplanting

  • Water the newly transplanted vines regularly, especially during dry spells.
  • Apply mulch around the base to retain moisture and suppress weeds.
  • Fertilize lightly in the spring with a balanced fertilizer to encourage growth.
column61: Containers or pots,Healthy riverbank grape vine,Plastic bags or a humidity dome,Potting soil,Rooting hormone,Sharp pruning shears,water

Begin by chilling the can of coconut milk in the refrigerator overnight. This will allow the cream to separate from the liquid. → Once the coconut milk has chilled, carefully open the can and scoop out the solidified cream into a mixing bowl, leaving the liquid behind. You can save the liquid for another use, such as adding it to smoothies. → In a high-speed blender, combine the soaked cashews, maple syrup, cocoa powder, vanilla extract, and a pinch of salt. Blend until smooth and creamy. → Add the coconut cream to the blender and blend again until everything is well combined and silky smooth. → Taste the mousse and adjust the sweetness if necessary by adding more maple syrup, one tablespoon at a time. → Transfer the mousse to individual serving glasses or a large serving dish. Chill in the refrigerator for at least 2 hours to allow it to set. → Before serving, garnish the mousse with fresh berries and mint leaves for a pop of color and freshness. → Enjoy this delectable vegan wedding mousse with your loved ones and savor every spoonful of its lusciousness!

Bake in the preheated oven for 20-25 minutes, or until a toothpick inserted into the center comes out with just a few moist crumbs. → Let the brownies cool completely in the pan before cutting them into squares. column32: The first step is to decide what type of cannabis infusion you’ll use. Most people use cannabis tincture or infused coconut oil for gummies because they mix easily with the other ingredients. (Tip: Decarboxylate your cannabis first to activate the THC or CBD!) → In a saucepan, pour your fruit juice and heat it over low-medium heat. Avoid boiling as high heat can degrade the cannabinoids. → Gradually sprinkle in the gelatin (or agar-agar) while whisking continuously to avoid clumps. Keep stirring until the mixture is smooth. → Once your mixture is smooth and slightly cooled (but still warm), add your cannabis tincture or infused oil. Stir thoroughly to ensure the cannabis is evenly distributed throughout the mixture. This step is crucial for consistent dosing in each gummy. → Using a dropper or small spoon, carefully fill your silicone molds with the gummy mixture. Work quickly as the mixture can start to set as it cools. → Place the filled molds in the refrigerator for at least 1-2 hours to allow the gummies to fully set. Once firm, pop them out of the molds.
